entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: A legume crop being improved through interspecific crosses.

entities.name: CROSS INCOMPATIBILITY
entities.description: A biological barrier in interspecific crosses where embryos abort after fertilization.

entities.name: EMBRYO RESCUE TECHNIQUES
entities.description: Techniques used to save aborting hybrid embryos to produce hybrid plants.

entities.name: GROWTH REGULATORS
entities.description: Chemical substances applied to pollinated pistils to prevent pod abscission.

entities.name: C. ARIETINUM
entities.description: A species of chickpea used as a parent in interspecific crosses.

entities.name: C. PINNATIFIDUM
entities.description: A wild species of chickpea used as a parent in interspecific crosses.

entities.name: GENOTYPE
entities.description: The genetic constitution of the parents affecting the success of hybrid production.

entities.name: LEAF STRUCTURE
entities.description: A morphological characteristic of the hybrid plants.

entities.name: GROWTH HABIT
entities.description: A morphological characteristic of the hybrid plants.

entities.name: FLOWER COLOUR
entities.description: A morphological characteristic observed in parents and hybrid plants.

entities.name: OVULE CULTURE
entities.description: A technique used to obtain hybrids from interspecific incompatible pollinations.

entities.name: EMBRYO CULTURE
entities.description: A technique used to obtain hybrids from interspecific incompatible pollinations.
